name: SSC (Shadowslice Snow Columns)
description: Shadowslice Snow Columns (SSC) is a hybrid method that combines orient-first concepts with column-based solving. It was developed as an experimental approach that orients pieces early in the solve, then uses a column-building strategy to complete the first two layers before finishing with a reduced last layer. SSC blends ideas from multiple method families into a unique solving philosophy.

steps.name: Edge Orientation + First Block Setup
steps.description: Orient edges and begin constructing a partial first block on the left side. Edge orientation is prioritized early to simplify later steps and enable efficient column building.
tips: Combine EO with block setup moves for efficiency

steps.name: Column Building
steps.description: Build columns (corner-edge pairs stacked vertically) to fill the first two layers. Columns are inserted using RU-based moves, taking advantage of the oriented edges from the first step.
tips: Oriented edges make column insertion smoother
Reduce rotations by inserting from multiple angles
Track remaining pieces to plan insertions

steps.name: Corner Orientation
steps.description: Orient the last layer corners. Since edges are already oriented from the first step, only corner orientation algorithms are needed.
tips: Only corner OLL cases apply (7 cases)
These are a subset of full OLL
Fast recognition since edges are already oriented

steps.name: Last Layer Permutation
steps.description: Permute all last layer pieces to their correct positions. With oriented pieces, this step uses PLL or can be split into corner and edge permutation.
tips: Oriented edges may reduce applicable cases

pros: Interesting hybrid of orient-first and blockbuilding
Good for developing versatile solving skills
cons: Very niche with a small community
Column building requires practice to execute efficiently
Not competitive with mainstream methods for speed
